source_documents: https://www.fatf-gafi.org (Trade-Based Money Laundering - Trends & Developments 2020; Risk Indicators 2021)
category: AML - trade-based money laundering via mis-invoicing and phantom trade
risk_signals: Invoice price or quantity materially misaligned with market or shipping documents
Goods described that do not match the trader's business or make no commercial sense
Multiple invoicing of the same goods, or circular/carousel trade between related parties
analyst_reasoning: Trade-based laundering moves value by misrepresenting trade: over- or under-invoicing shifts value across borders under cover of legitimate commerce, and phantom or multiple-invoiced shipments move money with no real goods. The tells are mismatches between invoices, shipping documents, and market prices.
investigation_steps: Reconcile invoice value and quantity against shipping documents and prevailing market prices
Check whether the traded goods fit the customer's declared business
Detect multiple invoicing, circular trade, and related-party carousel patterns
decision: File an STR, hold the trade-finance facility for document verification, and escalate for enhanced trade due diligence.
outcome: TBML hides in legitimate-looking trade volume; document-vs-market reconciliation is the primary detection method.
lessons_learned: Price/quantity mismatches against market and shipping docs are the core TBML indicators
Goods inconsistent with the trader's business signal phantom or cover trade
Multiple invoicing and carousel trade are classic value-transfer mechanisms
